---
title: "Basic Manual: Building Your Website in Quarto"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
---

## Purpose

This manual explains how to build and maintain your Quarto site at a practical level using the structure now in [`_quarto.yml`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/_quarto.yml), the theme in [`html/ath.scss`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/html/ath.scss), and the content patterns copied from the original site in [`/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-original`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-original).

The core idea is simple:

1. Quarto turns `.qmd` and `.md` files into web pages.
2. [`_quarto.yml`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/_quarto.yml) defines the site-level behavior.
3. Front matter at the top of each page controls page-specific behavior.
4. Listings let one page automatically display many child entries.
5. SCSS and templates control the styling and repeated layout patterns.

## Your Site Structure

The stripped-down version is organized like this:

```text
ath-quarto-kc/
  _quarto.yml
  _variables.yml
  index.qmd
  cv/
  intuition/
  news/
  research/
  teaching/
  talks/
  html/
  files/
  manuals/
```

What each part does:

- [`_quarto.yml`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/_quarto.yml): global site config, navbar, footer, theme, render behavior.
- [`_variables.yml`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/_variables.yml): reusable values such as years.
- [`index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/index.qmd): homepage and About page.
- [`cv/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/cv/index.qmd): CV page.
- [`intuition/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/intuition/index.qmd): listing page for your posts.
- [`news/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/news/index.qmd): listing page for short updates.
- [`research/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/research/index.qmd): listing page for research entries.
- [`teaching/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/teaching/index.qmd): listing page backed by YAML.
- [`talks/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/talks/index.qmd): listing page backed by YAML.
- [`html/ath.scss`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/html/ath.scss): your theme.
- [`html/blog/listing.ejs`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/html/blog/listing.ejs): blog/news list layout.
- [`html/research/listing.ejs`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/html/research/listing.ejs): research list layout.
- [`html/teaching/listing.ejs`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/html/teaching/listing.ejs): course card layout.
- [`html/talks/listing.ejs`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/html/talks/listing.ejs): talks list layout.
- [`files/`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/files): static assets like PDFs, bibliography files, icons, and images.

## How Quarto Pages Work

Every page begins with YAML front matter, like this:

```yaml
---
title: "About"
description: "Short summary"
---
```

Then below that comes the page body in Markdown or Quarto Markdown.

A minimal page looks like this:

```markdown
---
title: "My Page"
---

This is my page.

## Section

More content here.
```

## The Homepage / About Page

Your homepage is [`index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/index.qmd). It currently does two jobs:

- introduces you
- shows recent news automatically

To edit your bio, replace the text in the left column.

To keep the news feed working, leave this block in place:

```markdown
:::{#home-news}
:::
```

That placeholder is populated by the `listing:` settings in the page’s front matter.

## Navbar and Footer

The navbar and footer are set globally in [`_quarto.yml`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/_quarto.yml).

To add a page to the navbar, add another item under `website.navbar.left`:

```yaml
website:
  navbar:
    left:
      - text: "About"
        href: "index.qmd"
      - text: "Projects"
        href: "projects/index.qmd"
```

To change footer text, edit `website.page-footer`.

## Creating Standard Pages

If you want a normal standalone page like `projects`, `contact`, or `methods`, create a folder and add `index.qmd`:

```text
projects/
  index.qmd
```

Example:

```markdown
---
title: "Projects"
---

This page introduces my current projects.
```

Then add it to the navbar if you want it in the main menu.

## Writing Posts in Intuition

The Intuition section is the renamed blog.

The listing page is [`intuition/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/intuition/index.qmd).
New posts live in dated folders like this:

```text
intuition/
  2026/
    03/
      17/
        welcome/
          index.qmd
```

Use this as your template:

```markdown
---
title: "Post title"
date: 2026-03-17
description: "One-sentence summary for the listing page"
categories:
  - category-one
  - category-two
image: "img/card.png"
---

Your post starts here.
```

Use posts when you want:

- essays
- tutorials
- notes
- reflections
- technical explanations

## Adding Images to Posts or Pages

There are two common patterns.

### Pattern 1: Image beside a page

If a page uses a local image, keep the image in a nearby folder:

```text
intuition/2026/03/17/my-post/
  index.qmd
  img/
    chart.png
```

Then link it like this:

```markdown
![Chart showing results](img/chart.png)
```

### Pattern 2: Shared site asset

If the image should be reused across multiple pages, put it in [`files/`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/files):

```markdown
![Profile photo](/files/profile.jpg)
```

### Image tips

- Use relative paths like `img/chart.png` when the asset belongs to one page.
- Use root-style paths like `/files/profile.jpg` when the asset is shared.
- Set `image:` in front matter if you want thumbnails, cards, or social previews.

## Adding Links

### Normal link

```markdown
[My GitHub](https://github.com/yourname)
```

### Link to another page on your site

```markdown
[Research](/research/)
```

### Link to a PDF stored in your repo

Put the file in [`files/`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/files), then link it:

```markdown
[Download my CV](/files/cv.pdf)
```

## Code Blocks

Basic code block:

````markdown
```r
1 + 1
```
````

Executable R chunk:

````markdown
```{r}
summary(cars)
```
````

Executable Python chunk:

````markdown
```{python}
print("hello")
```
````

From the original site, you can also use Observable JS blocks for interactive browser-side graphics. Example pattern from one of the original blog posts:

````markdown
```{ojs}
Plot.plot({
  marks: [Plot.ruleY([0])]
})
```
````

Use OJS when you want charts to run in the browser without a server.

## Building a News Feed

Your homepage news feed is powered by files in [`news/`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/news).

To add an item, create a dated folder:

```text
news/2026/03/20/new-paper/
  index.qmd
```

Example:

```markdown
---
title: "New paper online"
date: 2026-03-20
description: "A short update about a new paper or event."
categories:
  - research
---

Longer note if you want one.
```

This will appear automatically:

- on the homepage via [`index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/index.qmd)
- on the full news page via [`news/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/news/index.qmd)

## Building the Research Section

The research section is driven by pages, not YAML. The listing page is [`research/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/research/index.qmd).

Each research item is its own page, for example:

```text
research/projects/my-paper/
  index.qmd
```

Use this front matter pattern:

```yaml
---
title: "Paper title"
date: 2026-03-17
categories:
  - public policy
  - methods
haiku:
  - One short line
  - Another short line
  - Final short line
pub-info:
  reference: "Cooper, Kai. 2026. \"Paper title.\" Working paper."
  links:
    - name: "Preprint"
      url: "paper.pdf"
      icon: fa-solid fa-file-pdf
      local: true
    - name: "Code"
      url: "https://github.com/yourname/project-repo"
      icon: fa-brands fa-github
---
```

Why this matters:

- `pub-info.reference` is what appears in the research listing.
- `pub-info.links` creates the buttons for PDF, DOI, code, appendix, or publisher links.
- `local: true` tells the template to look for the file in the same folder as the page.

If you want a downloadable paper PDF, store it in the same folder as the research page.

## Building the Teaching Section

The teaching page is different: it uses YAML data in [`teaching/courses.yml`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/teaching/courses.yml).

Each course entry looks like this:

```yaml
- title: Data Visualization
  description: "Course description here"
  university: Your institution
  number: COURSE 1234
  url: https://course-site.example.com
  logo: /files/placeholders/teaching-card.svg
  semester:
    - name: Spring 2026
      url: https://course-site.example.com
    - name: Fall 2026
```

This is useful when:

- the page is mainly structured metadata
- you want multiple cards in a consistent format
- each entry is short

## Building the Talks Section

Talks also use YAML data in [`talks/talks.yml`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/talks/talks.yml).

Example:

```yaml
- title: "Using Quarto for research websites"
  date: 2026-04-15
  description: "Invited talk for a methods workshop"
  location: Chicago, Illinois
  links:
    - name: Slides
      url: https://yourname.github.io/quarto-talk/slides.html
      icon: fa-solid fa-chalkboard-user
    - name: GitHub repository
      url: https://github.com/yourname/quarto-talk
      icon: fa-brands fa-github
```

This pattern is ideal for:

- conference talks
- workshops
- lectures
- panels
- podcasts

## Embedding PDFs, Downloads, and Other Files

For any file you want users to download:

1. store it in [`files/`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/files) or next to the page that uses it
2. link to it normally

Example:

```markdown
[Download syllabus](/files/syllabus.pdf)
```

If you want an embedded PDF page, the original CV page is a model. It used an iframe and a download button in [`/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-original/cv/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-original/cv/index.qmd).

## Feeds: RSS and Atom

Your site already has [`atom.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/atom.qmd) and [`rss.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/rss.qmd).

These create machine-readable feeds for Intuition posts.

You usually do not edit them often. You only need to ensure they point at the right content folder.

## Previewing Before Hosting

From the project root:

```bash
quarto preview
```

To render once:

```bash
quarto render
```

The rendered site goes into `_site/`.

## Hosting Options

### GitHub Pages

Good when:

- you want free static hosting
- you already use GitHub
- you want version control and public source code

Typical workflow:

1. put the site in a GitHub repository
2. render the site
3. publish `_site/` via GitHub Pages or GitHub Actions

### Netlify or Cloudflare Pages

Good when:

- you want easy drag-and-drop or git-based deploys
- you want custom domains and HTTPS with little setup

### Posit Connect

Good when:

- you want tighter R/Quarto integration
- you may eventually host interactive or scheduled content

### Manual server upload

The original site used a simple `rsync` deployment script in [`/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-original/deploy.sh`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-original/deploy.sh). That pattern is still valid if you control a server.

## Hosting Code Alongside the Site

A strong pattern from the original site is this:

- website page explains the project
- GitHub repository hosts the code
- site links to the repo
- talks and posts link out to slides, companion sites, and repositories

Recommended setup:

- one repo for your main website
- one repo per substantial talk, app, or tutorial
- link those repos from research pages, talks, or posts

Example links for a project page:

```yaml
pub-info:
  links:
    - name: Code
      url: https://github.com/yourname/project-name
      icon: fa-brands fa-github
    - name: Data
      url: https://osf.io/abcd1/
      icon: fa-solid fa-database
    - name: Preprint
      url: paper.pdf
      icon: fa-solid fa-file-pdf
      local: true
```

## A Good Content Workflow

For each new thing you publish, decide which bucket it belongs to:

- `news/`: brief update
- `intuition/`: reflective or tutorial-style post
- `research/`: paper or project page
- `teaching/`: course card or resource page
- `talks/`: presentation entry
- standalone page: permanent navigation page

## First Changes You Should Make

1. Replace the biography text in [`index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/index.qmd).
2. Replace the placeholder course in [`teaching/courses.yml`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/teaching/courses.yml).
3. Replace the placeholder talk in [`talks/talks.yml`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/talks/talks.yml).
4. Replace the starter research item in [`research/projects/site-template/index.qmd`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/research/projects/site-template/index.qmd).
5. Add your first real news item and first real Intuition post.
6. Add your social links and any extra navbar items in [`_quarto.yml`](/Users/kaicooper/Documents/ath-quarto-kc/_quarto.yml).

## Minimal Checklist for Every New Page

- clear `title`
- useful `description`
- correct `date` if it is time-based
- working links
- local files stored in the right folder
- image path correct
- categories added if the page is part of a listing
- page preview checked with `quarto preview`
